commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f3063b33e4b205748c51a1148b5377b3dc3d6685)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Android/Web realtime Talk handling, Web chat-thread transcript rendering, Android PCM playback, and gateway relay consult routing with focused tests.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction is attached. Source inspection shows current main forwards final user transcripts directly to the provider and the PR replaces that path with scheduled forced consults, so the compatibility issue is source-reproducible.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer decision on forced-consult policy plus contributor-supplied real Android/browser proof; automation cannot supply the missing real-environment evidence.

target.title: Stabilize realtime Talk turns, playback, and consults
target.body:
## Summary

- Serialize Android Gateway websocket frame handling and queue realtime PCM playback so assistant audio and transcript events are applied in order.
- Accumulate realtime transcript chunks and split alternating user/assistant turns correctly in Android and Web.
- Move Web realtime Talk bubbles into the main chat thread so voice turns flow downward like normal chat instead of living inside the composer.
- Force/route Web realtime consults through OpenClaw when the provider skips the consult, then keep Web gateway-relay answers behind the OpenClaw result instead of re-prompting Google with the raw user request.

## Root Cause

Android Gateway websocket frames were dispatched on separate coroutines, so realtime transcript/audio events could race. Android transcript deltas were also treated like full snapshots, which could leave only the latest assistant fragment visible, and PCM chunks were written directly from event handling with partial-write/choppy-playback risk.

The shared realtime conversation accumulator only closed the active user turn when an assistant turn began. It did not consistently close the active assistant turn when a genuinely new user turn began, so alternating turns could collapse into prior bubbles.

Web additionally rendered realtime Talk turns inside the composer/input box with a capped mini-scroll area. Even when the accumulator had separate entries, the layout did not behave like the main chat transcript.

For Web realtime consults, the provider can produce a final transcript without invoking `openclaw_agent_consult`. The relay now forces the consult in that case. The later live false-success report showed two consult tool calls, no final `tool.result`, then `session.closed`; matching session history showed the OpenClaw consult had started but had not produced a successful final action result. Code inspection showed the Web gateway relay also sent the recognized user sentence back into the realtime provider as a normal user message while the separate OpenClaw consult path was pending. That allowed the provider to speak from stale/provider context even when OpenClaw had not completed the tool-backed action.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Android realtime transcript/audio stability, Android/Web alternating Talk bubble separation, Web Talk bubble layout flow, Web realtime forced consult routing, and Web realtime provider false-success speech while OpenClaw consult is still pending or aborted.

Real environment tested: Focused Android unit-test/ktlint environment and focused Web/Node Vitest environment in the realtime Talk PR worktree. Live session history and relay logs from May 19, 2026 were inspected with personal identifiers and private task details omitted from the PR text.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.voice.TalkModeManagerTest`; `./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/realtime-talk-conversation.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.talk.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle-connect.node.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/styles/chat/layout.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts`; targeted `pnpm format:check`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: Android focused unit and ktlint checks passed. Web realtime conversation/view/lifecycle tests passed: 4 files, 51 tests. Web chat layout styles passed: 1 file, 8 tests. Talk consult/gateway tests passed: 3 files, 43 tests. Gateway realtime relay tests passed: 3 files, 27 tests. After reverting the exact-context guardrail commit, the combined focused talk suite passed: 6 files, 70 tests. `git diff --check` passed.

Observed result after fix: Android now keeps realtime events ordered, accumulates transcript chunks, closes old turns when the next speaker starts, and writes playback through a queued AudioTrack writer. Web now renders realtime Talk turns in the main chat thread, keeps alternating turns separate, auto-scrolls on realtime transcript updates, forces an OpenClaw consult when the realtime provider skips one, and no longer sends the raw recognized request back to the realtime provider before the OpenClaw consult result is ready. The provider only receives the existing checking/final prompts around the OpenClaw result path.

What was not tested: I did not run a fresh live browser realtime Talk session after the final Web relay patch, so I am not claiming the live hallucination is fully fixed yet. The focused regression proves the relay no longer sends the raw request to the provider on final transcript, which is the code path that matched the observed false-success behavior. I also did not independently inspect the Telegram screenshot artifact inside this PR. Live Android behavior was user-confirmed after the Android-side changes, but no fresh hardware recording is attached.

## Verification

- PASS: `./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.voice.TalkModeManagerTest`
- PASS: `./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/realtime-talk-conversation.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.talk.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle-connect.node.test.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/styles/chat/layout.test.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts`
- PASS: targeted `pnpm format:check`
- PASS: `git diff --check`
- NOT RUN: full Android app/device realtime Talk smoke and live browser realtime Talk smoke after the final Web relay patch.
